title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps · Runtime Lens: Feed Sitemap
summary: Navigation depends on filesystem order, tags differ by case, drafts leak into feeds, pagination duplicates pages, orphan content is invisible, and sitemap URLs disagree with canonical routes. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed sitemap increment in reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Derive feeds and sitemaps from the same published route manifest and validate absolute URLs, dates, escaping, exclusions, and counts.
Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-navigation-taxonomy-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-navigation-taxonomy-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_navigation_taxonomy_2(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"

        "reader15v65z",
        "source4560",
        "parsez56v51",
        "route15v65z4560",
        "artifact456015v65z",

    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"

stimulus.title: Feed Sitemap failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Feeds, sitemaps, and navigation may each discover content independently.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive feeds and sitemaps from the same published route manifest and validate absolute URLs, dates, escaping, exclusions, and counts.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the feed sitemap defect evidence boundary.

For repair the feed sitemap defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Feed Sitemap, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Feed Sitemap model · Theory Feed Sitemap
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Feed Sitemap: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7608 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive feeds and sitemaps from the same published route manifest and validate absolute URLs, dates, escaping, exclusions, and counts.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Feed Sitemap model · Theory Feed Sitemap.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Feed Sitemap model · Theory Feed Sitemap, keep Feed Sitemap connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive feeds and sitemaps from the same published route manifest and validate absolute URLs, dates, escaping, exclusions, and counts.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Feed Sitemap model · Theory Feed Sitemap, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Feed Sitemap precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Feed Sitemap model · Theory Feed Sitemap, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7608 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive feeds and sitemaps from the same published route manifest and validate absolute URLs, dates, escaping, exclusions, and counts.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Feed Sitemap model · Theory Feed Sitemap, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Feeds, sitemaps, and navigation may each discover content independently.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-feed-sitemap-15v65z.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-feed-sitemap-15v65z(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_feed_sitemap_15v65z\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?published < 1 or navigable < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?navigable != published)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?taxonomy_terms < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
